title: Add canonical run manifest JSON schema + validator; ship example manifests; add CI to prevent drift
preview: Context: For the Q1'26 Perf baseline & representative workload suite we need results to be reproducible and attributable across runs, regions, GPU SKUs, and scheduler/kernel changes. Today our benchmark harnesses emit slightly different “metadata blobs”, which makes run comparison brittle and blocks automated gating...
text: Context: For the Q1'26 Perf baseline & representative workload suite we need results to be reproducible and attributable across runs, regions, GPU SKUs, and scheduler/kernel changes. Today our benchmark harnesses emit slightly different “metadata blobs”, which makes run comparison brittle and blocks automated gating. This PR introduces the canonical “run manifest” format and enforcement tooling per ADR-0172 (benchmark metrics + run manifest schema). What’s in this PR: (1) JSON Schema for the canonical run manifest (schema id + semver) covering environment capture (driver/CUDA, OS image, container digest), runtime build identifiers, model identifiers/config, scheduler config pointers, workload definition + seed, run mode (streaming/non-streaming), and required tags (region, sku, cohort). (2) A small validator CLI that can be used locally and in CI to validate manifests (and optionally ...
links: Linear: ENG-4822 (Run manifest and results schema standardization)
Project: Q1 2026 Perf baseline & representative workload suite

title: Add rollback endpoint for Optimization Config versions (authz + audit events)
preview: Motivation: We need a safe, auditable way to revert optimization changes (batching/caching/quant profiles) without requiring a manual DB edit or redeploy. This PR adds the initial rollback API endpoint to the Optimization Config Service, including RBAC authorization, audit-log emission, and integration tests that co...
text: Motivation: We need a safe, auditable way to revert optimization changes (batching/caching/quant profiles) without requiring a manual DB edit or redeploy. This PR adds the initial rollback API endpoint to the Optimization Config Service, including RBAC authorization, audit-log emission, and integration tests that codify the expected semantics for callers (Console + internal tooling). API: Adds POST /v1/optimization-configs/rollbacks to request a rollback for a given scope (tenant/model/region) to a target config_version_id (or "previous_promoted" shortcut). The endpoint creates a rollback operation record, performs server-side validation (scope match, version exists/visible, cooldown, and active rollout compatibility), and returns an operation_id that the rollout controller/runtime applier will observe. Authz: Enforces new permission "optimization_config.rollback" and validates caller...

title: Add SLO gate evaluation hooks + required metric tags for optimization config rollouts
preview: Summary: This PR adds the first-cut SLO gate evaluation hooks used by the optimization config safe-apply rollout controller, and standardizes required metric tags so we can reliably compute gates and annotate dashboards. Motivation: During the rollback gameday + earlier incident review, we saw that gate evaluation w...
text: Summary: This PR adds the first-cut SLO gate evaluation hooks used by the optimization config safe-apply rollout controller, and standardizes required metric tags so we can reliably compute gates and annotate dashboards. Motivation: During the rollback gameday + earlier incident review, we saw that gate evaluation was brittle because rollouts were missing consistent labels (tenant/model/region/config_version) and different components emitted slightly different tag keys. This makes it hard to attribute p95/p99 latency and 5xx spikes to a specific optimization config version while a canary is in-flight. What’s in this PR (high level): (1) Gate hook interface: RolloutController now calls a GateEvaluator at each stage boundary (pre-canary, canary step, promote) to decide whether to continue/pause/fail. The evaluator is intentionally side-effect free and returns structured reasons to displ...

title: Add canary rollout controller for Optimization Config (progressive apply + pause/resume)
preview: Context: Optimization Config changes (batching heuristics, cache toggles, quantization profiles) have caused production regressions and mis-scoped rollouts (see SUP-18503). As part of the Safe Apply + Rollback project, we need a first-class controller that can progressively apply a config version to a target scope a...
text:
Context: Optimization Config changes (batching heuristics, cache toggles, quantization profiles) have caused production regressions and mis-scoped rollouts (see SUP-18503). As part of the Safe Apply + Rollback project, we need a first-class controller that can progressively apply a config version to a target scope and gate progression on health signals, with the ability to pause/resume and handle failures deterministically.

What this PR adds: (1) Canary rollout controller service inside the control plane that orchestrates staged apply for Optimization Config versions (tenant/model/region scope) using percent-based shards. (2) Pause/resume/cancel APIs and state machine persistence so rollouts survive process restarts. (3) Failure handling that stops progression, records the reason, and leaves the system in a safe paused state (no automatic rollback in this PR; rollback wiring is in EN...

title: Add perf-canary alert handler (Slack threads, dedupe, runbook links)
preview:
Summary: Adds a first-class handler in incident-bot for perf-canary regression alerts. The handler formats alerts into a consistent Slack thread, de-dupes repeated firings, and attaches deep links (Grafana dashboard + Confluence runbook + relevant traces) to speed triage.

Context: The perf-canary service will emit ...
text:
Summary: Adds a first-class handler in incident-bot for perf-canary regression alerts. The handler formats alerts into a consistent Slack thread, de-dupes repeated firings, and attaches deep links (Grafana dashboard + Confluence runbook + relevant traces) to speed triage.

Context: The perf-canary service will emit alerts as a webhook payload (model + region + tier + metric + observed vs baseline + confidence + baseline version/runtime tags). Before this PR, incident-bot treated canary payloads as generic webhooks which led to noisy, non-threaded posts and no reliable dedupe key, making it hard to triage during regressions and especially during baseline drift/threshold tuning.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a new webhook route + handler for source=perf-canary (Content-Type: application/json). 2) Builds a stable dedupe key and thread key so repeat evaluations (same model/metric/region/sev...

title: Add perf-canary gates for hardware profile rollouts (latency + throughput) and adjust alert routing
preview: Context: As part of the hardware tuning profiles pack rollout (H100/L40S first, B200 gated), we need perf-canary to catch regressions that only show up when `hardware_profile_pack` is enabled and the runtime selects SKU-specific presets (scheduler/kernels/KV-cache budgets). Existing canary jobs compare runtime build...
text:
Context: As part of the hardware tuning profiles pack rollout (H100/L40S first, B200 gated), we need perf-canary to catch regressions that only show up when `hardware_profile_pack` is enabled and the runtime selects SKU-specific presets (scheduler/kernels/KV-cache budgets). Existing canary jobs compare runtime builds against a baseline, but they don’t segment by “profile-enabled” vs “profile-disabled”, and alerts are currently too noisy (especially on L40S runners per INT-8832). This PR adds explicit gates for profile-enabled builds, tightens/loosens thresholds where appropriate, and updates alert routing so actionable regressions page the right rotation.

What’s in this PR: (1) New gate group `hardware_profiles_rollout` that runs paired experiments (A/B) with `RUNTIME_ENABLE_HARDWARE_PROFILE_PACK=1` (treatment) vs `=0` (control) for the same build SHA where possible, and evaluates de...

title: Add dashboard panels for hardware profile selection + tail latency regressions
preview: Context: As we roll out the hardware tuning profile pack (H100/L40S first; B200 gated), oncall and perf owners need to answer two questions quickly: (1) which profile was actually selected (and why), and (2) did enabling the profile cause a tail-latency regression in a specific pool/region/model/workload slice. This...
text:
Context: As we roll out the hardware tuning profile pack (H100/L40S first; B200 gated), oncall and perf owners need to answer two questions quickly: (1) which profile was actually selected (and why), and (2) did enabling the profile cause a tail-latency regression in a specific pool/region/model/workload slice. This PR updates our Grafana dashboards in observability-pack to add segmentation by `hardware_profile` (selected profile name + version) and `hardware_profile_source` (fleet default vs per-pool vs per-deployment override), and adds focused panels for tail behavior (p95/p99) with rollout-friendly breakdowns.

What’s included (high level): Adds a new dashboard row “Hardware profile selection” to the runtime/inference overview dashboard, showing selected profile distribution, profile source distribution, and a table of “top offenders” (pool/region/model) where p95/p99 is above thr...

title: Add KMS throttle budget + request backpressure to prevent KMS saturation under spikes
preview: Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope...
text:
Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope ops (e.g. cache cold start + traffic spike) could saturate KMS and extend the incident blast radius.

This PR introduces an explicit "KMS throttle budget" and integrates KMS calls with the existing request admission/backpressure path so we shed/queue work earlier rather than stampeding KMS.

What’s included (high level): (1) Per-process token-bucket budget for KMS operations: A lightweight budget that is decremented on KMS requests and refilled at a configured rate. When depleted, KMS calls are delayed (bounded) or rejected with a typed error depending on caller policy. Th...

title: Document KMS error taxonomy in code and logs
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds inline documentation and concrete conventions for how we represent KMS failures in logs and traces. The goal is to make triage consistent across services (control plane + data plane) and to ensure dashboards/alerts can rely on stable field names.

This is intentionally “docs + conventions + g...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds inline documentation and concrete conventions for how we represent KMS failures in logs and traces. The goal is to make triage consistent across services (control plane + data plane) and to ensure dashboards/alerts can rely on stable field names.

This is intentionally “docs + conventions + guardrails”, not functional behavior changes. It complements the wrapper work in pr-18421 and metrics/spans work in pr-18434.

Linked: ENG-4822 (taxonomy + metrics tags), ENG-4823 (wrapper).

## What changed
- Adds a canonical KMS error taxonomy enum (with mapping notes) and documents when each category is used.
- Defines structured log field names + required fields for all KMS failures.
- Documents how errors should be surfaced to operators (what’s safe to include, what must be redacted).
- Adds a small helper that ensures required KMS log fields are present when logging wr...

title: Add config guardrails for KMS timeout/retry budgets (prod-safe defaults + explicit override)
preview:
Context: As part of the KMS hardening work (ADR-007) and following the recent near-miss retry amplification behavior, we need a hard stop on “retry storm by config.” We currently allow operators to dial up timeouts/retries in config, which in prod can turn a transient KMS degradation into cascading request pileups.
...
text:
Context: As part of the KMS hardening work (ADR-007) and following the recent near-miss retry amplification behavior, we need a hard stop on “retry storm by config.” We currently allow operators to dial up timeouts/retries in config, which in prod can turn a transient KMS degradation into cascading request pileups.

What this PR does: Introduces validation + guardrails for KMS client config so overly aggressive timeout/retry settings cannot be enabled in production without an explicit, time-bound override. This is enforced in two places: (1) control-plane config validation when publishing/rolling out config; (2) service startup validation as a backstop for manually edited configs/private installs.

Guardrails (prod): - kms.timeout_ms must be within [200, 2000]. - kms.max_retries must be within [0, 4]. - kms.max_retry_elapsed_ms must be within [0, 5000]. - computed “worst-case budget” ...

title: hotfix(kms): reduce retry amplification on timeouts + add jitter
preview: Context: We had a near-miss on 2025-11-16 where transient KMS timeouts caused a retry storm and elevated 5xx due to retry amplification across layers (gateway -> control-plane client -> kms wrapper). This PR is a production hotfix to reduce amplification, introduce jitter, and ensure we respect an end-to-end retry b...
text:
Context: We had a near-miss on 2025-11-16 where transient KMS timeouts caused a retry storm and elevated 5xx due to retry amplification across layers (gateway -> control-plane client -> kms wrapper). This PR is a production hotfix to reduce amplification, introduce jitter, and ensure we respect an end-to-end retry budget so we don’t turn a dependency blip into a load event. Incident thread: slack://incidents/1747779033-near-miss-kms-retry-storm (related earlier spike: slack://incidents/1746508855-kms-timeout-spike-us-east). Customer correlation: SUP-19377.

What changed (high level): 1) Enforce a single retry budget per request for KMS decrypt/envelope operations (attempts + time). 2) Switch timeout retry backoff to exponential w/ full jitter (AWS recommended) and cap max sleep. 3) Stop retrying once the call is past the request deadline (previously we could start a new attempt with <...

title: Refactor key fetch caching to avoid stale material during KMS rotation
preview:
## Context
During the KMS hardening/rotation work (ENG-4821/ENG-4823), we found that the current key fetch path can return stale key material after a rotation or key state change. The root issue is that we were effectively using one cache for multiple concepts (key metadata vs derived material) and the cache key did...
text:
## Context
During the KMS hardening/rotation work (ENG-4821/ENG-4823), we found that the current key fetch path can return stale key material after a rotation or key state change. The root issue is that we were effectively using one cache for multiple concepts (key metadata vs derived material) and the cache key did not include sufficient version/state.

This showed up as intermittent decrypt failures shortly after rotation cutovers (and was correlated with KMS throttling in SUP-19377, since retries + stale cache amplified the blast radius).

## What this PR does
- Splits caching into two layers with explicit semantics:
 1) **Key metadata cache**: KMS key identity + state (e.g., KeyId/Arn, enabled/disabled, primary version marker where applicable). Short TTL; safe to refresh.
 2) **Key material cache**: derived/usable material for envelope operations (e.g., resolved wrapping key handl...

title: control-plane: add KMS rotation dual-read/dual-decrypt window (config-flagged)
preview: Context: We need a safe, auditable KMS key rotation path that avoids hard cutovers and reduces the blast radius of misconfig / partial rollout. This PR introduces a control-plane dual-read/dual-decrypt window so services can continue to decrypt material encrypted under the previous key version while we gradually rew...
text:
Context: We need a safe, auditable KMS key rotation path that avoids hard cutovers and reduces the blast radius of misconfig / partial rollout. This PR introduces a control-plane dual-read/dual-decrypt window so services can continue to decrypt material encrypted under the previous key version while we gradually rewrap / cut over. This is a control-plane-only change (no new crypto primitives). Behavior is controlled via config flags and aligns with ADR-008: sources/confluence/architecture-and-standards/decision-records/adr-008-key-rotation-strategy-dual-read-and-cutover.json

What this changes: (1) Adds a "keyset" concept to control-plane KMS config resolution that can return both primary (new) and secondary (old) key version references when dual-read is enabled; (2) Updates envelope decrypt flows that originate in the control plane (e.g., decrypting stored secrets / DEK blobs) to att...

title: Add overload error classification rules (separate from 5xx) for Hosted API SLO reporting
preview: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding rollout, the gateway and downstream services now emit stable overload semantics (HTTP 429/503 + a Redwood error code + Retry-After). Our current SLO classifier treats most 5xx as availability failures, which makes overload-induced shedding look like an ...
text:
Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding rollout, the gateway and downstream services now emit stable overload semantics (HTTP 429/503 + a Redwood error code + Retry-After). Our current SLO classifier treats most 5xx as availability failures, which makes overload-induced shedding look like an outage and obscures the intent of graceful degradation. This PR updates slo-toolkit classification rules so overload errors are tracked separately from generic 5xx while still being visible in dashboards/alerts.

What this changes: Adds a new classifier bucket `overload` and rules to map requests into it based on (1) the canonical Redwood error code (from `x-redwood-error-code` header or JSON body `error.code`) and (2) the overload HTTP status mapping (429/503 with overload code). Removes these responses from the default 5xx classifier so they no longer inflate `server_erro...

title: Add least-privilege KMS IAM modules + separate rotation vs runtime decrypt roles
preview:
## Summary
This PR introduces least-privilege IAM policy modules for AWS KMS usage in Private/Dedicated Terraform, and explicitly separates the principal used for day-to-day envelope operations (decrypt/encrypt via KMS) from the principal used for rotation/admin tasks (CreateAlias/UpdateAlias, Enable/DisableKey, Sch...
text:
## Summary
This PR introduces least-privilege IAM policy modules for AWS KMS usage in Private/Dedicated Terraform, and explicitly separates the principal used for day-to-day envelope operations (decrypt/encrypt via KMS) from the principal used for rotation/admin tasks (CreateAlias/UpdateAlias, Enable/DisableKey, ScheduleKeyDeletion, key policy updates, etc.).

This is in support of the KMS integration hardening initiative. We’ve seen multiple customer environments where the runtime workload role is over-permissioned (kms:* on *), which both fails security review and increases blast radius for credential compromise. We’ve also had real-world decrypt failures caused by IAM policy drift and incorrect principal assumptions (see SUP-19341 for an example).

## Goals
- Provide composable, reviewed IAM policy documents for the common KMS patterns we support.
- Enforce role separation between:...

title: Add KMS dependency healthcheck endpoint + SRE hooks (predeploy gate)
preview:
Summary
- Adds an internal KMS dependency healthcheck endpoint for services that rely on envelope decryption.
- Exposes standardized SRE hooks so rollout tooling can gate deploys on KMS readiness and SRE can alert on sustained degradation.

Motivation / context
We’ve had a few cases (and a recent us-east timeout spi...
text:
Summary
- Adds an internal KMS dependency healthcheck endpoint for services that rely on envelope decryption.
- Exposes standardized SRE hooks so rollout tooling can gate deploys on KMS readiness and SRE can alert on sustained degradation.

Motivation / context
We’ve had a few cases (and a recent us-east timeout spike) where KMS latency/throttling started degrading request paths, but we didn’t have a single, low-noise signal that answers: “is KMS healthy enough for this service to safely take traffic right now?”

This PR implements an internal healthcheck that:
- does NOT call KMS on each request (avoids amplification / self-inflicted throttling)
- distinguishes transient vs sustained failures
- standardizes output so alerting + predeploy checks can consume it consistently

Design highlights
- Endpoint: GET /internal/health/kms
 - Returns 200 when KMS is healthy per local probe state
...

title: Add actionable errors for KMS PermissionDenied (missing IAM perms / wrong principal / wrong key ARN)
preview:
## Summary
This PR improves KMS PermissionDenied/AccessDenied error handling so operators get actionable, environment-specific remediation hints instead of a generic "AccessDenied" blob.

Motivation: We’ve had repeated support/on-call escalations where decrypt failures were caused by (a) missing specific IAM permiss...
text:
## Summary
This PR improves KMS PermissionDenied/AccessDenied error handling so operators get actionable, environment-specific remediation hints instead of a generic "AccessDenied" blob.

Motivation: We’ve had repeated support/on-call escalations where decrypt failures were caused by (a) missing specific IAM permissions, (b) requests being executed under an unexpected principal (IRSA/instance profile mismatch), or (c) key ARN mismatch (wrong region/account/alias). See SUP-19341 and the KMS hardening epic.

This change focuses on message quality + structured context while preserving security posture (no secret material, no policy dumping). It also standardizes log fields so triage dashboards/runbooks can key off consistent dimensions.

## User-visible behavior
When KMS returns AccessDenied (or equivalent) we now:
- Emit a structured error with a short human-readable summary.
- Attach a...

title: Add shared KMS client abstraction with explicit timeouts + standardized retries
preview: Summary: Introduces a shared KMS client abstraction used across control-plane + data-plane services to standardize timeouts, retries/backoff, and error mapping. The current KMS call sites implement timeouts inconsistently (or not at all) and several retry policies amplify load during provider-side latency spikes, wh...
text:
Summary: Introduces a shared KMS client abstraction used across control-plane + data-plane services to standardize timeouts, retries/backoff, and error mapping. The current KMS call sites implement timeouts inconsistently (or not at all) and several retry policies amplify load during provider-side latency spikes, which contributed to elevated 5xx during throttling events.

Key goals: - Make KMS dependency behavior predictable (bounded latency, bounded retries) - Provide a canonical error taxonomy for downstream policy (circuit breaker + degrade-mode followups) - Improve observability: metrics + structured log fields to enable on-call triage

Scope of this PR: - New library package `pkg/kmsclient` with: - explicit deadlines for Encrypt/Decrypt/GenerateDataKey/DescribeKey (per-operation defaults) - retry policy w/ exponential backoff + jitter and a hard retry budget (max attempts and ma...

title: Add KMS metrics + tracing spans for standardized wrapper calls
preview: Summary: Instruments all KMS operations invoked via the new shared wrapper with metrics and trace spans following Redwood telemetry conventions. Adds latency histograms, request/error counters (tagged by canonical error taxonomy), and a consistent tracing span shape so on-call can correlate KMS degradation to elevat...
text: Summary: Instruments all KMS operations invoked via the new shared wrapper with metrics and trace spans following Redwood telemetry conventions. Adds latency histograms, request/error counters (tagged by canonical error taxonomy), and a consistent tracing span shape so on-call can correlate KMS degradation to elevated 5xx/latency. Motivation: As we harden KMS integration paths (ENG-4821), we need consistent KMS dependency signals across control-plane and data-plane components. Recent support cases and a throttling-related incident (SUP-19377) showed we lacked enough granularity to quickly answer: (1) which operation is failing, (2) whether it’s retry amplification, (3) what error class dominates, and (4) which region/provider is impacted. This PR builds on pr-18421’s wrapper API and wires metrics/spans at the wrapper boundary (single place, consistent tags). What’s included: Metrics (...

title: Add KMS latency/error dashboards + alert rules (per env/region)
preview: Context: As part of the KMS integration hardening initiative we needed first-class dependency visibility for KMS across Hosted/Dedicated/Private paths. This PR adds a KMS Dependency Health dashboard and alert rules that align to the canonical error taxonomy (AccessDenied/NotFound/Throttling/Timeout/InvalidCiphertext...
text: Context: As part of the KMS integration hardening initiative we needed first-class dependency visibility for KMS across Hosted/Dedicated/Private paths. This PR adds a KMS Dependency Health dashboard and alert rules that align to the canonical error taxonomy (AccessDenied/NotFound/Throttling/Timeout/InvalidCiphertext/Unknown) and our metric namespacing conventions. Scope: (1) Dashboard: Overview + per env/region panels for request volume, latency (p50/p95/p99), error rate by category, throttling signals, and circuit breaker state; (2) Alerts: page-worthy vs ticket-worthy thresholds, with environment-aware routing (prod pages, staging tickets) and burn-rate style windows where appropriate; (3) Operator ergonomics: runbook links + log/trace pivot hints (trace_id, kms_key_id, provider, region, service); (4) Documentation: short README explaining how to use/tune the alerts and what to chec...

title: Implement KMS circuit breaker + fallback mode to prevent retry storms during KMS incidents
preview: Context: During recent KMS throttling/timeouts (see SUP-19377), application-side retries amplified load and created a request storm against KMS, worsening tail latency and elevating 5xx. We’ve already standardized timeouts/retries in the KMS wrapper (PR #18421); this change adds circuit breaking + a documented degra...
text:
Context: During recent KMS throttling/timeouts (see SUP-19377), application-side retries amplified load and created a request storm against KMS, worsening tail latency and elevating 5xx. We’ve already standardized timeouts/retries in the KMS wrapper (PR #18421); this change adds circuit breaking + a documented degrade/fallback policy so we fail fast and shed load instead of stampeding KMS.

What this PR does:
- Adds a per-key (key ARN) circuit breaker in the shared KMS client wrapper with CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN states.
- Introduces a fallback mode policy for decrypt/envelope operations:
 - If circuit is OPEN: do not call KMS; attempt to serve from in-memory DEK cache if present (bounded TTL, non-persistent); otherwise fail fast with a mapped KMSUnavailable error (no retries).
 - Encrypt/new key generation paths: fail fast when OPEN (no cache fallback), returning a distinct error class ...

title: Enforce readiness gate before adding nodes to serving pools
preview:
## Summary
This PR enforces a readiness gate in the control plane so newly provisioned GPU nodes cannot be added to serving pools (Dedicated or shared) until they have passed provision-to-ready validations and are in `Ready` state. This closes a gap we saw during early rollout where nodes could join the pool after k...
text:
## Summary
This PR enforces a readiness gate in the control plane so newly provisioned GPU nodes cannot be added to serving pools (Dedicated or shared) until they have passed provision-to-ready validations and are in `Ready` state. This closes a gap we saw during early rollout where nodes could join the pool after kubelet join but before validations (GPU health / driver parity / NCCL smoke / perf canary) completed, causing avoidable brownouts and noisy remediation.

The gate is feature-flagged and supports a rollback toggle. It also includes safety checks to avoid deadlocking pool scale-ups if readiness signals are missing/late.

## Motivation / Context
- During the staged rollout we observed instances where the autoscaler/pool controller saw a node as schedulable and added it to a serving pool while it was still in `Validating`, leading to:
 - GPU driver drift errors surfacing as run...

title: Stability gates: auto-rerun on p95 variance threshold + flaky-run CI coverage
preview: Context: We’re seeing run-to-run instability in p95 latency on some model/region pairs (queueing effects, transient noisy neighbors, kernel selection differences). This PR adds a stability gate to the benchmark pipeline that detects high variance in p95 latency and automatically re-runs the affected slice up to a bo...
text: Context: We’re seeing run-to-run instability in p95 latency on some model/region pairs (queueing effects, transient noisy neighbors, kernel selection differences). This PR adds a stability gate to the benchmark pipeline that detects high variance in p95 latency and automatically re-runs the affected slice up to a bounded limit. Goal: reduce noise in published aggregates while preserving the raw evidence trail (we keep the original run results and annotate what happened). What’s included: (1) Variance detection on repeated trials for each (model, region, workload, concurrency) slice, based on p95 latency. (2) Auto rerun when variance exceeds a configurable threshold, with a cap on reruns and explicit status reporting. (3) Manifest + result artifacts include stability metadata (variance %, trials, rerun_count, stability_status, stability_notes). (4) CI coverage: simulated flaky runs wit...

title: Add Private upgrade health dashboards (prechecks, migrations, error rates, rollbacks)
preview: Summary: Adds a Grafana dashboard pack for Redwood Private upgrade health, focused on the new installer-driven upgrade workflow (prechecks -> backup -> control plane rollout -> data plane rollout -> migrations -> postchecks, with rollback entry points). Dashboard set covers (1) precheck failure rates + breakdown by ...
text: Summary: Adds a Grafana dashboard pack for Redwood Private upgrade health, focused on the new installer-driven upgrade workflow (prechecks -> backup -> control plane rollout -> data plane rollout -> migrations -> postchecks, with rollback entry points). Dashboard set covers (1) precheck failure rates + breakdown by check_id/severity, (2) DB migration durations and timeouts/lock waits, (3) API/control-plane error rates during upgrade windows, and (4) rollback frequency + outcomes. Motivation: SRE and Release Eng asked for a single place to validate upgrade readiness and spot regressions during beta rollouts; today we rely on scattered logs and ad-hoc queries. This PR makes upgrades observable with consistent panels and variables that work in Hosted VPC Private and on-prem (including air-gapped) where only Prometheus + Loki are available. What’s included: - New dashboard: "Private Upgra...

title: Annotate upgrade-window incidents + link to Private release artifacts
preview:
## Summary
When a Private upgrade is running (VPC or on-prem), we want incidents that fire in that window to be clearly labeled and automatically correlated to the exact release artifacts involved.

This PR updates incident-bot’s enrichment pipeline to:
- detect upgrade windows based on a canonical source (release t...
text:
## Summary
When a Private upgrade is running (VPC or on-prem), we want incidents that fire in that window to be clearly labeled and automatically correlated to the exact release artifacts involved.

This PR updates incident-bot’s enrichment pipeline to:
- detect upgrade windows based on a canonical source (release train + installer upgrade state signals)
- tag incidents created during those windows as upgrade-related
- attach links to relevant release artifacts (release tag, build provenance, and installer plan bundle) so on-call can quickly determine whether a spike is likely upgrade-induced vs. product regression.

This is part of the "Private upgrade + rollback mechanism" workstream focused on auditability and reduced MTTR.

## Motivation
We’ve repeatedly had to manually correlate incidents during maintenance windows:
- Was there an upgrade running?
- Which Private version / chart ...

title: Hotfix: prevent burst starvation under telemetry lag
preview: Context: During Dedicated burst beta we observed brief "denial storms" where one tenant would get disproportionately denied burst admissions for ~30–90s after a telemetry delay event. This manifested as burst capacity being available (per fleet dashboards) but the admission controller continuing to deny with reason=...
text: Context: During Dedicated burst beta we observed brief "denial storms" where one tenant would get disproportionately denied burst admissions for ~30–90s after a telemetry delay event. This manifested as burst capacity being available (per fleet dashboards) but the admission controller continuing to deny with reason=POOL_SATURATED / FAIRNESS_THROTTLE for that tenant, while other tenants recovered faster. Root cause: the fairness + pool-availability calculation in the admission controller was using a raw (unsmoothed) free-capacity signal derived from fleet telemetry. When telemetry arrived late/out-of-order, the computed free_capacity could transiently drop to ~0 and stay pinned until the next sample; the token-bucket refill path treated that as hard saturation and stopped refilling, effectively starving tenants that had recently spent tokens. Fix: (1) add EWMA smoothing and monotonic g...

title: Rollout canary safety smoke tests (cohorting + auto-stop wiring) for staging
preview: Summary: This PR adds a small suite of staging-only safety smoke tests for Hosted canary rollouts. The goal is to catch two classes of beta issues before they reach customers: (1) cohorting / split enforcement regressions (e.g., observed split mismatch per region) and (2) stop-condition wiring regressions (e.g., SLO...
text:
Summary: This PR adds a small suite of staging-only safety smoke tests for Hosted canary rollouts. The goal is to catch two classes of beta issues before they reach customers: (1) cohorting / split enforcement regressions (e.g., observed split mismatch per region) and (2) stop-condition wiring regressions (e.g., SLO breach evaluates but orchestrator doesn’t pause/rollback, or rollout step stalls on metric timeouts). These tests are intentionally narrow, deterministic, and cleanup-safe so they can be run as part of GA change management.

Motivation / context: During beta we saw an incident-like split mismatch in us-east (SUP-48377) and a separate class of failures where metric evaluation timeouts could stall progress (see follow-ups in ENG-9158 / pr-28895). We already have E2E coverage in the monorepo (ENG-9164), but we wanted a lightweight “canary of the canary” that runs against stag...

title: Add e2e integration tests with Splunk HEC + Datadog Logs sandboxes
preview: Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need repeatable end-to-end confidence that the exporter can deliver to downstreams with realistic failure modes (auth errors, 429 throttling, payload validation issues) and that our retry/backoff + checkpointing semantics behave as expected. This PR adds an integr...
text: Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need repeatable end-to-end confidence that the exporter can deliver to downstreams with realistic failure modes (auth errors, 429 throttling, payload validation issues) and that our retry/backoff + checkpointing semantics behave as expected. This PR adds an integration test suite + docker-compose-ish harness that boots the exporter alongside (a) a Splunk-like HEC stub service and (b) a Datadog Logs intake stub service. It does not attempt to fully emulate Splunk/Datadog; it’s focused on request/response behavior the adapters depend on. Scope: - New `e2e/` harness with compose config for local + CI runs - Splunk HEC stub: validates token header, supports `/services/collector/event` + `/services/collector/health`, can simulate 429s and partial failures - Datadog stub: validates API key header, supports `/api/v2/logs`, can simulate 403...

title: Add Audit Exporter Grafana dashboards + alert rules (lag, delivery errors)
preview: Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need first-class operational visibility for the audit-log-exporter service (lag, retries, adapter failures) and alerting aligned to the SLOs defined by SRE. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for the exporter, intended to be deployed to...
text: Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need first-class operational visibility for the audit-log-exporter service (lag, retries, adapter failures) and alerting aligned to the SLOs defined by SRE. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for the exporter, intended to be deployed to hosted + dedicated environments for the beta cohort. Links: Linear ENG-5168 (exporter observability), Confluence SLO doc: "Audit exporter SLOs and alerting" (owner: Sean/Rafael). Scope: (1) Grafana dashboard: Overview (lag, throughput, success rate), (2) Grafana dashboard: Destination adapters (Splunk HEC / Datadog / S3), (3) Alert rules: export lag burn, sustained adapter errors, DLQ growth, auto-paused exports, (4) Recording rules for rate-based panels to reduce query cost. Notes on semantics: Export lag is computed from exporter checkpoint watermark vs event time; alert...

title: Add runbook links to experiment alerts + Console guardrail banners
preview: Problem: During beta we saw multiple slow triage loops where the on-call (and Support) had to ask around for the right runbook when an experiment guardrail alert fired (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant skew, etc.). We already have runbooks in Confluence, but the link isn’t present where the incident s...
text: Problem: During beta we saw multiple slow triage loops where the on-call (and Support) had to ask around for the right runbook when an experiment guardrail alert fired (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant skew, etc.). We already have runbooks in Confluence, but the link isn’t present where the incident starts: alert payloads and the Console guardrail banners. This PR wires canonical runbook URLs into (1) alert payload annotations (so PagerDuty/Slack notifications carry a direct ‘Runbook’ link) and (2) Console guardrail banners on the experiment details/results pages. Scope: route experiments only (no global SRE alert conventions refactor in this PR). What changed: - Added a small, versioned mapping of {alert_key -> runbook_url} for experiment-related alerts (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant 0 traffic, allocation skew, p99 regression auto-stop). - Plumbed run...

title: Add exporter metrics (lag/retries/throughput), basic tracing, and structured logs
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-pass Prometheus metrics + basic tracing + structured logging to the audit-log-exporter service. Metrics are aligned to Redwood observability naming/cardinality standards (per Allison/Logan feedback) and provide the signals needed for initial dashboards/alerts (lag, throughput, retries, ad...
text: Summary: This PR adds first-pass Prometheus metrics + basic tracing + structured logging to the audit-log-exporter service. Metrics are aligned to Redwood observability naming/cardinality standards (per Allison/Logan feedback) and provide the signals needed for initial dashboards/alerts (lag, throughput, retries, adapter errors). This work is scoped to exporter-side instrumentation only; the control plane status endpoint will consume these signals via existing health paths and the observability pack dashboards will be done separately. Context: ENG-5168. Motivation: During beta soak we’ve been debugging exporter lag spikes and downstream 429/backpressure mostly via ad-hoc logs. We need stable metrics to (a) quantify export lag vs SLO, (b) separate downstream throttling from internal queueing, (c) support paging policy (page on sustained lag / hard failures, ticket on transient retries)...

title: Reduce metrics cardinality for route experiment dimensions (limited tags + sampled expansions)
preview: Context: As we ramp Route-level Experiments beta traffic, we’ve seen two recurring issues: (1) high time-series explosion when tagging high-volume metrics with {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, model_id, region}, and (2) query cost spikes in Console results views that join on raw route identifiers. This PR adjus...
text:
Context: As we ramp Route-level Experiments beta traffic, we’ve seen two recurring issues: (1) high time-series explosion when tagging high-volume metrics with {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, model_id, region}, and (2) query cost spikes in Console results views that join on raw route identifiers. This PR adjusts the default metrics tagging strategy to keep the canonical attribution (experiment_id + variant_id) while constraining high-cardinality dimensions and providing a sampled path for deeper drilldowns.

What changed (high level): We now treat experiment attribution as two tiers: Tier A (always-on) and Tier B (sampled expansion). Tier A is used for all request + token hot-path counters/histograms and includes: experiment_id, variant_id, route_norm_id (normalized/template route identifier, not raw path), and deployment_mode (hosted|dedicated|private). Tier B is emitted at a c...

title: sdk(go): add streaming reconnect helper + example for resilient chat streams
preview: Context: We’ve seen a recurring onboarding/first-prod issue where customers try streaming in a quickstart-like environment and interpret transient disconnects (mobile networks, reverse proxies, idle timeouts) as SDK instability. Support ticket SUP-18488 captures a representative failure mode. This PR adds a small, o...
text:
Context: We’ve seen a recurring onboarding/first-prod issue where customers try streaming in a quickstart-like environment and interpret transient disconnects (mobile networks, reverse proxies, idle timeouts) as SDK instability. Support ticket SUP-18488 captures a representative failure mode. This PR adds a small, opinionated helper for resilient streaming consumption in the Go SDK and a runnable example to show recommended patterns.

What’s included: (1) A new streaming reconnect helper in package streamx that wraps our existing streaming client and provides controlled reconnect behavior. It retries on network/transport errors and selected HTTP statuses (408/429/5xx) with exponential backoff + jitter, respects Retry-After, and uses a bounded max elapsed time / max attempts. (2) A lightweight event cursor that lets callers preserve progress (last received event ID / last chunk index) ...

title: Add rollout cohort panels (latency + errors) and alert templates
preview: Context: As part of Hosted canary rollout GA work, we need a standardized dashboard/alert baseline that makes it easy to compare baseline vs canary cohorts (latency + errors) without every team re-deriving PromQL and without blowing up cardinality. This PR adds a small set of reusable panels + a “rollout overview” d...
text: Context: As part of Hosted canary rollout GA work, we need a standardized dashboard/alert baseline that makes it easy to compare baseline vs canary cohorts (latency + errors) without every team re-deriving PromQL and without blowing up cardinality. This PR adds a small set of reusable panels + a “rollout overview” dashboard to the observability pack, plus alert templates and recommended default thresholds for auto-stop/rollback wiring. What’s included: (1) New dashboard: “Hosted Rollouts / Cohort Comparison (Latency + Errors)” with panels for request rate, error rate, timeout rate, p50/p95 latency, and a baseline-vs-canary delta view. Panels are parameterized around {route, region, rollout_id} and assume cohort in {baseline, canary}. (2) Library panels (Jsonnet) that can be embedded into other service dashboards: p95 latency by cohort, 5xx rate by cohort, timeouts by cohort, and “cana...

title: Security: destination validation + egress allowlist hooks (SSRF/exfil mitigation)
preview: Motivation: This PR adds a defense-in-depth validation layer in the exporter to reduce SSRF and data-exfiltration risk by (a) validating destination endpoints for Splunk HEC and Datadog prior to use and (b) adding an egress allowlist hook that can be enforced by the runtime environment. We already validate some dest...
text: Motivation: This PR adds a defense-in-depth validation layer in the exporter to reduce SSRF and data-exfiltration risk by (a) validating destination endpoints for Splunk HEC and Datadog prior to use and (b) adding an egress allowlist hook that can be enforced by the runtime environment. We already validate some destination fields in the control plane, but we want the exporter to be safe even if a bad config slips through (migration bugs, stale cached config, or future destinations). Summary of changes: 1) Strict URL parsing + normalization: Introduce a shared DestinationURL parser that rejects ambiguous/invalid URLs, strips userinfo, normalizes scheme/host/port, and prevents path tricks (e.g., embedded @, backslashes, encoded host separators). 2) Blocklist for dangerous targets: Reject localhost, link-local, and RFC1918 / private ranges (including 127.0.0.0/8, ::1, 10.0.0.0/8, 172.16....

title: Add cache hit-rate dashboard panels + initial alert rules (conservative thresholds)
preview: Summary: Adds first-pass dashboard panels for prefix/KV cache efficiency and supporting signals (eviction pressure + wasted prefill), and introduces conservative alert rules intended as guardrails during rollout. Motivation: With runtime instrumentation landing in the monorepo (cache hit/miss counters, eviction/pres...
text: Summary: Adds first-pass dashboard panels for prefix/KV cache efficiency and supporting signals (eviction pressure + wasted prefill), and introduces conservative alert rules intended as guardrails during rollout. Motivation: With runtime instrumentation landing in the monorepo (cache hit/miss counters, eviction/pressure metrics, wasted prefill), SRE needs a standard triage surface to answer: (1) did hit-rate actually drop, (2) why, and (3) is it eviction/pressure vs config/policy vs workload mix. This PR is intentionally conservative on paging; most alerts are ticket-level until we tune against real workload patterns. What’s included (Dashboards): 1) Cache Efficiency Overview (new row): KV cache hit-rate (time series) with filters: deployment_mode, region, model_family, route_group; prefix cache reuse effectiveness (reused_tokens / prefill_tokens) as both time series and heatmap by pr...

title: Add ingestion-side cardinality guards for cache telemetry
preview:
## Summary
This PR implements ingestion-side cardinality guards for the new prefix/KV cache telemetry emitted by the runtime and tagged by the gateway. The goal is to prevent accidental series explosions (per-tenant/per-route/per-request-shape label combinations) while preserving the ability to break down cache sign...
text:
## Summary
This PR implements ingestion-side cardinality guards for the new prefix/KV cache telemetry emitted by the runtime and tagged by the gateway. The goal is to prevent accidental series explosions (per-tenant/per-route/per-request-shape label combinations) while preserving the ability to break down cache signals by the dimensions we actually operate on (model, route, deployment mode, region, and prompt-length/token bins).

This is the ingestion counterpart to runtime tagging + allowlists (see redwood/pr-18426) and ensures that even if a client or misconfigured gateway emits unexpected label keys/values, we either (a) rewrite to a safe aggregated bucket, or (b) drop with explicit counters so we can diagnose.

## Context / Motivation
During early canary of cache telemetry dashboards we saw a few failure modes:
- `tenant_id` and raw `route` occasionally showing up due to legacy ga...

title: Add cache telemetry notes + flags to runtime release notes
preview:
## Summary
This PR updates the runtime release notes to document the new prefix/KV cache telemetry landing in this train, including:
- What new metrics exist and how to interpret them at a high level
- The config/feature flags required to enable the new signals safely

text:
## Summary
This PR updates the runtime release notes to document the new prefix/KV cache telemetry landing in this train, including:
- What new metrics exist and how to interpret them at a high level
- The config/feature flags required to enable the new signals safely
- Known limitations / caveats for the first production rollout

This is intended to reduce confusion during staged enablement (staging -> canary -> prod) and to make it clear which dashboards/alerts are expected to move when these flags are flipped.

## Context
Cache telemetry work has been landing across multiple PRs (counters, histograms, miss reasons, evictions/pressure, wasted prefill, sampling/guards, trace annotations, naming reconciliation). We’ve had multiple internal questions during canary setup about:
- whether hit-rate is expected to move immediately (and how sampling affects it)
- what labels should/shouldn’...

title: Add cache telemetry overhead perf-canary gate (p95 regression threshold)
preview: Adds a new perf-canary workload and CI gate to quantify runtime overhead from the prefix/KV cache telemetry instrumentation. The canary runs a stable request mix with caching enabled and toggles telemetry flags to compare p95 end-to-end latency (prefill+decode) against a baseline. If the measured p95 regression exce...
text:
Adds a new perf-canary workload and CI gate to quantify runtime overhead from the prefix/KV cache telemetry instrumentation. The canary runs a stable request mix with caching enabled and toggles telemetry flags to compare p95 end-to-end latency (prefill+decode) against a baseline. If the measured p95 regression exceeds the configured threshold, the canary fails to prevent rollouts that silently add latency overhead.

Motivation: We’re rolling out new cache metrics (hit/miss counters, eviction pressure, wasted prefill) behind flags and with sampling, but we need a hard stop in CI to keep instrumentation overhead near-zero (especially at p95/p99). This PR is intended to be the guardrail referenced by the runtime rollout plan and SRE approval thread.

What’s included: (1) New scenario `cache_telemetry_overhead` that replays a prompt set designed to exercise prefill, decode, prefix reuse,...

title: Alerts: experiment config propagation lag + attribution anomalies (route experiments)
preview: Context: Route-level experiments rely on control-plane config propagation into the routing/policy layer. During beta we observed occasional propagation lag and a few classes of attribution anomalies (missing experiment_id/variant_id tags, variant receiving ~0 traffic due to config/eligibility issues, and split skew ...
text: Context: Route-level experiments rely on control-plane config propagation into the routing/policy layer. During beta we observed occasional propagation lag and a few classes of attribution anomalies (missing experiment_id/variant_id tags, variant receiving ~0 traffic due to config/eligibility issues, and split skew early in rollout). This PR adds Prometheus/Alertmanager rules and annotations for the standard route experiments dashboards, with SRE-reviewed runbook links and recommended severities. Scope: (1) propagation lag/staleness alerts tied to the new propagation lag metric emitted by the redwood monorepo, (2) attribution drop-rate and missing-dimension alerts for experiment metrics/traces aggregation, (3) “variant 0 traffic” detection to catch misconfigured allocations or eligibility rules. Notes: rules are written to avoid cardinality blow-ups (no per-route fanout by default); h...

title: Fix missing experiment attribution on streaming retries
preview: Context: During the route experiments beta we saw a subset of streaming requests (SSE) lose experiment attribution after a retry, which caused (a) undercounted per-variant traffic, (b) misleading guardrail charts, and (c) confusing traces where the initial attempt was tagged but the retry attempt was not. This was e...
text: Context: During the route experiments beta we saw a subset of streaming requests (SSE) lose experiment attribution after a retry, which caused (a) undercounted per-variant traffic, (b) misleading guardrail charts, and (c) confusing traces where the initial attempt was tagged but the retry attempt was not. This was escalated via SUP-24877 and corroborated by staging attribution-drop dashboards. Root cause: Our streaming retry path reconstructs a new RequestContext / SpanContext for the retry attempt. The base routing decision (including experiment assignment) was persisted on the first attempt, but the retry code path re-evaluated portions of the request pipeline and did not copy experiment dimensions into the retry context. In some cases we also emitted the streaming response headers from the retry attempt before experiment tags were re-attached, so x-redwood-experiment-id / x-redwood...
links: Jira SUP-24877: Missing experiment attribution on streaming
Slack thread: #support escalation referencing missing experiment_id on SSE retries
Grafana: Route Experiments / Attribution Drop Rate dashboard (staging)

title: Add retention verification + compliance pack generation dashboards/alerts
preview: Summary: This PR adds the first version of the retention/compliance dashboards and alerting rules needed for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” program. It introduces (1) Retention Enforcement Verification dashboards (TTL lag, purge throughput/failures, backlog) and (2) Compliance Report Pack Gener...
text:
Summary: This PR adds the first version of the retention/compliance dashboards and alerting rules needed for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” program. It introduces (1) Retention Enforcement Verification dashboards (TTL lag, purge throughput/failures, backlog) and (2) Compliance Report Pack Generation SLIs (success rate + latency) with corresponding alerts. It also updates the repo README to document metric prerequisites and rollout guidance.

Context / why: We’ve had multiple incidents/tickets where enforcement was “working” but not observable at the right level (e.g., policy applied but TTL lag growing in a single region; purge jobs silently throttling after maintenance). Security/GRC also needs a customer-ready way to evidence enforcement (at least internally) and to demonstrate the compliance pack generator’s reliability as we start using it for enterprise secu...

title: Dedicated throughput SLO: add dashboard panels for floor tracking, saturation, and drilldowns
preview: Context: We’re rolling out a formal Dedicated throughput SLO and need a consistent, low-friction dashboard experience for SRE/CS and (later) a curated subset for customers. This PR extends the dedicated mixin dashboards with panels that (a) track throughput floors over time per customer/deployment, (b) show saturati...
text:
Context: We’re rolling out a formal Dedicated throughput SLO and need a consistent, low-friction dashboard experience for SRE/CS and (later) a curated subset for customers. This PR extends the dedicated mixin dashboards with panels that (a) track throughput floors over time per customer/deployment, (b) show saturation signals that correlate with SLO misses, and (c) provide drilldowns into the exact deployment/model/region slice.

What’s included: 1) “Throughput floor vs actual” panels: tokens/sec (per deployment_id) overlaid with computed floor (tier + shape) and SLO evaluator compliance; includes 5m/30m rollups with clear “below floor” highlighting. 2) “Saturation signals” row: admission-control shed rate, queue depth, in-flight requests, batcher wait time, KV cache hit ratio (when available), and GPU utilization proxy (SM occupancy / active time from node exporter where present). 3)...

title: Dedicated throughput SLO: add burn-rate alerts + wire to paging policies
preview: Context: As part of Dedicated throughput guarantees, we need first-class SLO alerting that is (a) burn-rate based (fast/slow), (b) scoped per Dedicated deployment, and (c) correctly routed to oncall + CS escalation paths. This PR adds burn-rate alert rules for the Dedicated Throughput Floor SLO and wires them into o...
text: Context: As part of Dedicated throughput guarantees, we need first-class SLO alerting that is (a) burn-rate based (fast/slow), (b) scoped per Dedicated deployment, and (c) correctly routed to oncall + CS escalation paths. This PR adds burn-rate alert rules for the Dedicated Throughput Floor SLO and wires them into our paging policies. It also adds the runbook links and dashboard deep-links to make the alerts actionable. Scope: - New Prometheus rules for Dedicated throughput SLO burn-rate (page + ticket) - Tier-aware routing labels (Standard/Plus/Premium) to support different urgency/paging - Alertmanager routes + PagerDuty service mapping (SRE oncall; non-page tickets to #customer-success triage) - Annotations: customer/deployment identifiers, region, model, and links to the Dedicated Throughput dashboard panels - Guardrails: suppress alerts when deployment is in provisioning/warmup w...

title: Telemetry: cardinality guardrails for unit economics tags
preview:
Summary
- Adds hard validation + enforcement guardrails for high-cardinality tag dimensions used by the Unit Economics pipeline (model_id/model_version, region, customer_tier, quantization_profile, route_group, streaming).
- Prevents accidental explosions in metrics cardinality (e.g. route_id, raw customer identifie...
text:
Summary
- Adds hard validation + enforcement guardrails for high-cardinality tag dimensions used by the Unit Economics pipeline (model_id/model_version, region, customer_tier, quantization_profile, route_group, streaming).
- Prevents accidental explosions in metrics cardinality (e.g. route_id, raw customer identifiers, arbitrary quantization profile strings) that have previously caused ingestion lag and warehouse view instability.
- Emits explicit counters for dropped/rewritten tags so we can alert before dashboard coverage degrades.

Context
The internal Unit Economics dashboard depends on stable metering dimensions (see Confluence: token-metering-dimensions-and-cardinality). Over the last few weeks we’ve seen several near-misses where a single deploy introduced unbounded values in a tag (route id with embedded customer UUID; quantization profile leaking experiment run ids), which th...

title: Add Private backup/restore dashboards + alert rules (backup success, duration, restore drill outcomes)
preview: Context: As part of the Private Day-2 backup/restore toolkit, we need an opinionated observability pack that SRE/Solutions can deploy into customer VPC/on-prem environments to answer two questions quickly: (1) are backups happening and completing successfully on schedule, and (2) are restore drills passing and meeti...
text: Context: As part of the Private Day-2 backup/restore toolkit, we need an opinionated observability pack that SRE/Solutions can deploy into customer VPC/on-prem environments to answer two questions quickly: (1) are backups happening and completing successfully on schedule, and (2) are restore drills passing and meeting RTO targets in staging. This PR adds a set of dashboards and alert rules designed to work across hosted VPC installs and constrained on-prem/air-gapped setups (where available metrics are limited and log shipping may be disabled). Scope: (A) dashboards for backup job success rate, last_success_ts, duration, bytes, and error categories; (B) dashboards for restore drill outcomes (pass/fail, duration, validation phase breakdown) and drill cadence; (C) alert rules with conservative defaults plus documented tuning for noisy environments (slow object storage, intermittent KMS ...
links: Linear: ENG-9826 (metrics + alerts), ENG-9827 (restore drill automation)

title: Dedicated: emit burst-capacity metering events (committed vs burst) for billing exports
preview: Context: Dedicated tiers require billing/usage export to separate committed throughput consumption from burst capacity so Finance/Sales/CS can explain invoices and enforce tier policies (ENG-19302). This PR introduces a canonical metering event for burst usage, wires it into the request accounting path, and adds a d...
text: Context: Dedicated tiers require billing/usage export to separate committed throughput consumption from burst capacity so Finance/Sales/CS can explain invoices and enforce tier policies (ENG-19302). This PR introduces a canonical metering event for burst usage, wires it into the request accounting path, and adds a daily aggregation job that produces committed_vs_burst totals per deployment_id/model/region. Definitions: (1) committed_usage_tokens = tokens attributed while the deployment is within its committed throughput floor capacity slice, (2) burst_usage_tokens = tokens attributed while the deployment consumes burst pool capacity above its committed slice, (3) unmetered_tokens = tokens excluded from billing exports (e.g., internal health checks, warmup). Attribution is computed at request finalization using the tier-aware admission/routing decision already attached to the request c...

title: Routing: map Dedicated tier → pool/shape selector (feature-flagged, safe defaults)
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a tier-aware pool/shape selector to the router so Dedicated requests can be mapped deterministically to a hardware pool (and optionally a shape) based on customer tier (Standard/Plus/Premium) and region availability. The change is gated behind a feature flag and defaults to current routing be...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a tier-aware pool/shape selector to the router so Dedicated requests can be mapped deterministically to a hardware pool (and optionally a shape) based on customer tier (Standard/Plus/Premium) and region availability. The change is gated behind a feature flag and defaults to current routing behavior when tier metadata is missing or the selector cannot find a compatible target.

Key goals:
- Provide a single canonical mapping tier → pool/shape used by the control plane + router.
- Make behavior safe-by-default (no tier info or config mismatch should not break routing).
- Enable gradual rollout via feature flag + shadow evaluation metrics.

Related: ENG-19263 (Tier-aware routing policy tier→pool mapping).

## Motivation / context
Dedicated throughput SLOs require consistent capacity accounting and predictable placement. Today, Dedicated deployments may route to wh...

title: [LTS 1.18] Fix Sev1 crash in KV cache compaction (backport)
preview:
Context
This is a targeted backport to runtime-1.18-lts for a Sev1 crash in KV cache compaction reported during LTS pilot validation and via SUP-18544. The crash manifests as a use-after-free / invalid access during compaction when a block is concurrently scheduled for eviction under memory pressure. We are treating...
text:
Context
This is a targeted backport to runtime-1.18-lts for a Sev1 crash in KV cache compaction reported during LTS pilot validation and via SUP-18544. The crash manifests as a use-after-free / invalid access during compaction when a block is concurrently scheduled for eviction under memory pressure. We are treating this as LTS-eligible (correctness + crash) and release-blocking for 1.18.1-lts RC.

Root cause
Compaction iterates over a segment list while eviction can detach/free segments after a memory-pressure callback. On main, this was mitigated by refcounted pinning and a stricter lock ordering (compaction acquires segment_lock before eviction_lock). In the 1.18 LTS branch, we still had the older code path where compaction relied on "best-effort" pointer stability and a non-atomic "in_compaction" flag, which is insufficient under bursty workloads with aggressive eviction.

Fix
- I...

title: Add time-windowed exports + actor/event-type filters (report templates)
preview:
## Summary
This PR enhances `audit-log-exporter` to support compliance/evidence workflows by adding (1) explicit time-windowed exports and (2) optional filters for actor + event types. It also introduces a small report-template layer so the compliance reporting pack can request a known preset ("last_90_days_admin_ac...
text:
## Summary
This PR enhances `audit-log-exporter` to support compliance/evidence workflows by adding (1) explicit time-windowed exports and (2) optional filters for actor + event types. It also introduces a small report-template layer so the compliance reporting pack can request a known preset ("last_90_days_admin_activity", "rbac_changes", etc.) without re-encoding filter logic in multiple places.

Motivation: enterprise security reviews are consistently asking for "audit logs for a defined period" and "only admin/security-relevant events". We also had a support report (SUP-3917) where customers believed exports were missing event types; part of that was unclear defaults and lack of an explicit event-type filter list in the export request.

## API changes
### New request fields (non-breaking)
- `start_time` (RFC3339, inclusive)
- `end_time` (RFC3339, exclusive)
- `actor_ids` (optional...
